scheduleReferences.title: President's Daily Diary Entry, January 21, 1991
scheduleReferences.scopeAndContentNote: In a busy day at the White House, President Bush met with his senior national security and defense advisors about ongoing hostilities in the Persian Gulf. He also briefed members of Congress, including House Speaker Thomas Foley of Washington. Later in the day, he met with the former Prime Minister of Singapore. Throughout the day, the President made numerous telephone calls to world leaders, including the Prime Ministers of Spain, Italy, and Turkey.

scheduleReferences.title: President's Daily Diary Entry, January 30, 1991
scheduleReferences.scopeAndContentNote: The President met with his senior staff for his morning national intelligence and security briefings. Following these meetings, the President had his morning meeting with Chief of Staff John Sununu and telephoned Turkish President Turgat Ozal. He then met with Antonios Samaras, Foreign Minister of Greece. Later in the morning, he traveled to Capitol Hill where he attended the 50th anniversary of President Franklin D. Roosevelt's Four Freedoms Speech. He also met with Congressional leadership including Senator Robert Dole and Congressman Bob Michel. In the evening, the President dined with the Reverend Billy Graham.

researchNote: PDF extent: 4 pages. Digital object: 1991-02-05--Ozal.pdf. Catalog: https://catalog.archives.gov/id/428081419.

scheduleReferences.title: President's Daily Diary Entry, February 5, 1991
scheduleReferences.scopeAndContentNote: Prior to his morning national security and intelligence briefings, the President played tennis with Chris Evert. Later in the morning, he spoke by telephone with President Carlos Salinas of Mexico, Prime Minister Brian Mulroney of Canada, and President Turgat Ozal of Turkey. Meetings with his domestic policy staff including Roger B. Porter followed. Before lunch, the President held his 71st news conference for members of the press. In the afternoon, the President spoke with President Francois Mitterand of France, and met with senior advisors to discuss the Highway Reauthorization Bill. In the evening after dinner, President Bush spoke with Margaret Thatcher and Walter H. Annenberg.

scheduleReferences.title: [Presidential Daily Diary] 2/15/1991
scheduleReferences.scopeAndContentNote: In the morning, the President received his national security and intelligence briefings. He then met with members of the American Association for the Advancement of Science. In the late morning, the President traveled aboard Air Force One to Massachusetts and then spoke to employees of the Raytheon Plant, makers of the Patriot Missile. He then participated in a Patriot Missile simulation. From there, the President traveled to Kennebunkport, Maine.

documentTitle: Telcon with President Turgut Ozal of Turkey on February 19, 1991

source.name: National Archives Catalog
source.collection: Records of the National Security Council (George H. W. Bush Administration)
source.series: Presidential Telcon Files

frusVolume.title: Foreign Relations of the United States, 1989-1992, Volume VI, Eastern Mediterranean

sourceNote: Source: George H.W. Bush Library, Bush Presidential Records, National Security Council, Presidential Telcon Files, Telcon with President Turgut Ozal of Turkey on February 19, 1991. Release status: Full. NAID 428081469.
researchNote: PDF extent: 4 pages. Digital object: 1991-02-19--Ozal.pdf. Catalog: https://catalog.archives.gov/id/428081469.

scheduleReferences.title: [Presidential Daily Diary] 2/19/1991
scheduleReferences.scopeAndContentNote: Back at the White House after a weekend at Kennebunkport, the President met with his national security and intelligence advisors. He then met with his senior advisors to discuss Operation Desert Storm. Before lunch, he participated in a photo opportunity with members of the American Association of Engineering Societies. He also held a meeting with his domestic policy staff including Roger B. Porter and Lamar Alexander, Secretary of Education. Following lunch, the President spoke on a conference call with Francois Mitterand, President of France and met with Richard G. Darman, Director of the Office of Management and Budget. He then met with the Secretary of the Interior Manuel Lujan, Jr., to discuss the opening of the Arctic National Wildlife Refuge.

scheduleReferences.title: [Presidential Daily Diary] 2/26/1991
scheduleReferences.scopeAndContentNote: With the Persian Gulf War nearing an end, the President continued to speak daily with heads of state around the world. He began this day with a conversation by telephone with Vaclav Havel, President of Czechoslovakia. He then had his morning national security and intelligence briefings and met with senior advisors to discuss Operation Desert Storm. At 9:48 a.m., the President addressed the nation to announce Iraq's withdrawal from Kuwait. Following these remarks, members of Congress were briefed by the President on fast-track authority. Prior to lunch, he met with Cesar Gaviria Trujillo, President of Columbia. The afternoon consisted of additional meetings on Operation Desert Storm with Colin L. Powell and Richard B. Cheney in attendence.
